title: polygala alpestris subsp. croatica
intro: Polygala alpestris subsp. croatica, a subspecies of the Polygala alpestris plant, is a type of perennial herb belonging to the milkwort family. It is native to the mountainous regions of Croatia and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant contains flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. In herbal preparations, Polygala alpestris subsp. croatica is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to alleviate respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
